assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 6 File 1 (line 150-151): "Reuse apply_filter from common/src/db/query.rs: Use the apply_filter function to handle parsing the comma-separated values and generating the SQL IN clause. This function already handles both single-value and multi-value comma-separated parameters, so no new parsing logic is needed." Also referenced in Step 4 (line 70-71) and Step 6 File 2 (line 160). reuse-analysis.md Section 1 (lines 9-25) provides detailed explanation of apply_filter reuse for comma-separated parsing.

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 6 File 1 (line 139): "Following the pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs where the severity query parameter is defined as an optional field in the Query struct". Step 4 (lines 86-87): "modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs -- structurally identical endpoint (list with optional filter). Examine the Query struct pattern, how severity is extracted, how the filter is threaded from handler to service." reuse-analysis.md Section 2 (lines 29-42) details the advisory pattern as a structural reference for Query struct, handler extraction, validation, and error response patterns.

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 6 File 2 (lines 158-160): "Join the package table with the package_license table using the entity defined in entity/src/package_license.rs (SeaORM entity)." Step 4 (lines 76-77): "entity/src/package_license.rs (reuse candidate) - Use mcp__serena_backend__get_symbols_overview to see the entity struct and its SeaORM column definitions, which are needed for the JOIN query." reuse-analysis.md Section 3 (lines 46-63) explicitly states "Using the existing entity is both safer and more maintainable than raw SQL" and describes using SeaORM entity for JOIN construction.

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md contains three numbered sections covering all candidates: Section 1 (lines 9-25) covers apply_filter with reuse type, usage location, purpose, and rationale; Section 2 (lines 29-42) covers advisory list pattern with 4 sub-points on what to replicate; Section 3 (lines 46-63) covers package_license entity with 3 sub-points on what it provides. Each section includes Reuse type, Usage location, Purpose, and Rationale fields. The summary table on lines 80-86 lists all three primary candidates.
